issue: My Outlook account says password incorrect
resolution:
Problem Summary:
My Outlook account is showing an incorrect password, and I've tried changing it on the web but it's not working.

Likely Cause:
The password may have expired or is too weak, causing the system to reject it.

Step-by-Step Resolution:
Step 1. Navigate to the Microsoft Password Reset Portal using a supported browser or app (e.g., Chrome, Edge, Teams, etc.).
Step 2. Enter your company email address.
Step 3. Create a new password that is strong (use special characters, avoid personal info, and don't repeat previous passwords).
Step 4. Check your account settings to ensure the correct email and device are used.
Step 5. Verify the new password by logging into Outlook Web at https://outlook.office.com.

issue: I forgot my Outlook password
resolution:
Problem Summary:
I forgot my Outlook password

Likely Cause:
Your password has expired. You need to update it.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Open the MFA Setup Portal: https://aka.ms/mfasetup

Step 2.
Create a new password with a strong combination of letters, numbers, and special characters.

Step 3.
Enter the new password in the second field and confirm it.

Step 4.
Review your account activity to ensure there are no unauthorized actions.

Step 5.
Open Outlook Desktop and sign back into the account.

issue: I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in. I need urgent access as I have an important meeting in 30 minutes.
resolution:
Problem Summary:
I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in due to an important meeting in 30 minutes.

Likely Cause:
The account is locked due to multiple failed login attempts, possibly due to an expired password or security measures.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Open any web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari) and navigate to: https://account.live.com/password/reset and press Enter.

Step 2.
After resetting the password, Outlook will automatically detect the change and prompt you for new credentials. Enter your company email and the new password.

Step 3.
If you're on a company device, tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox. Click OK to reconnect to Outlook.

Step 4.
If the connection is slow, use Outlook Web Access immediately by opening https://outlook.office.com, entering your company email and new password, and signing in.

Step 5.
If still locked out, change the password again, enable MFA, contact IT Security, and review activity logs for further assistance.

issue: I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in. I need urgent access as I have an important meeting in 30 minutes.
resolution:
Problem Summary:
The user has forgotten their Outlook email password and is unable to log in, requiring urgent access for an important meeting.

Likely Cause:
The account lockout resolution process must be followed due to multiple failed login attempts, necessitating immediate action to regain access.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Open any web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari) and navigate to the Microsoft Password Reset Portal: https://account.live.com/password/reset.

Step 2.
Create a new password that meets the following criteria: at least 8 characters long, containing at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. Ensure it is not the same as any of your last 5 passwords and does not contain personal information.

Step 3.
After creating the password, confirm it on the Microsoft Password Reset Portal.

Step 4.
Open Outlook Desktop and confirm the new password. Outlook should automatically detect the change and prompt for new credentials.

Step 5.
If the password reset does not work, navigate to Outlook Web Access: https://outlook.office.com, sign in with your company email and new password, and attempt to access your account.

Step 6.
If account lockout occurs, immediately go to the lockout resolution page: https://account.live.com/activity, review recent sign-in attempts, and take necessary steps to regain access.

Step 7.
If issues persist, contact IT Security immediately by emailing security@company.com with details of the incident, including the link clicked and the new password used.

issue: I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in. I need urgent access as I have an important meeting in 30 minutes.
resolution:
Problem Summary:
I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in.

Likely Cause:
The user has forgotten their Microsoft 365 email password, preventing them from logging into Outlook Desktop, Outlook Web Access (OWA), Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, or any other Microsoft 365 application.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Go to https://account.live.com/password/reset

Step 2.
Follow the prompts to reset your password. The new password must: Be at least 8 characters long (12 or more is recommended). Contain at least one uppercase letter (example: A, B, C). Contain at least one lowercase letter (example: a, b, c). Contain at least one number (example: 1, 2, 3). Contain at least one special character (example: !, @, #, $, %). Not be the same as any of your last 5 passwords. Not contain your name or email address. A strong password example: MyCompany@2026Secure! Confirm the password in the second field and click Next.

Step 3.
Sign Back Into Outlook Desktop: Open Outlook Desktop on your computer. Outlook will automatically detect the password change and prompt for new credentials. Enter your company email address and the new password you just created. Tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox if on a company device. Click OK. Outlook will reconnect to the server — this may take 1-2 minutes.

Step 4.
Emergency Access for Urgent Meetings: If Outlook Desktop is taking too long to sync after a password reset and you have an urgent meeting or email to access, use Outlook Web Access immediately. Open your browser and go to: https://outlook.office.com. Click Sign In and enter your company email and new password. You have immediate access to all emails, calendar, contacts, and meeting links through the web interface.

issue: I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in.  I need urgent access as I have an important meeting in 30 minutes.
resolution:

I forgot my Outlook email password and cannot log in.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Go to https://account.live.com/password/reset

Step 2.
Follow the prompts to reset your password. The new password must: Be at least 8 characters long (12 or more is recommended). Contain at least one uppercase letter (example: A, B, C). Contain at least one lowercase letter (example: a, b, c). Contain at least one number (example: 1, 2, 3). Contain at least one special character (example: !, @, #, $, %). Not be the same as any of your last 5 passwords. Not contain your name or email address. A strong password example: MyCompany@2026Secure! Confirm the password in the second field and click Next.

Step 3.
Sign Back Into Outlook Desktop: Open Outlook Desktop on your computer. Outlook will automatically detect the password change and prompt for new credentials. Enter your company email address and the new password you just created. Tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox if on a company device. Click OK. Outlook will reconnect to the server — this may take 1-2 minutes.

Step 4.
Emergency Access for Urgent Meetings: If Outlook Desktop is taking too long to sync after a password reset and you have an urgent meeting or email to access, use Outlook Web Access immediately. Open your browser and go to: https://outlook.office.com. Click Sign In and enter your company email and new password. You have immediate access to all emails, calendar, contacts, and meeting links through the web interface.

Step 5.
If the Report Message button is not visible, forward the email as an attachment to: phishing@company.com. To forward as attachment in Outlook, go to Home > More > Forward as Attachment.

Step 6.
Delete the email from your inbox and empty the Deleted Items folder.

Step 7.
Repair Office: Close all Office apps. Go to Control Panel > Programs > Microsoft 365 Apps > Change > Quick Repair. Takes 5 minutes without internet. If Quick Repair fails, use Online Repair (requires internet, takes 15-30 minutes).

Step 8.
Repair Outlook Profile: Go to Control Panel > Mail > Show Profiles > Add. Create a new profile, add your email account, set as default. This creates a fresh Outlook configuration.

issue: I forgot my Outlook password
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Employee has forgotten their Microsoft 365 email password and cannot log into Outlook Desktop, Outlook Web Access (OWA), Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, or any other Microsoft 365 application.

Likely Cause:
The employee has forgotten their password, leading to inability to authenticate and access their account.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Navigate to Microsoft Password Reset Portal: Open any web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari). In the address bar, type exactly: https://account.live.com/password/reset and press Enter.

Step 2.
Enter Your Email Address: On the password reset page, enter your company email address in the field provided and click Next.

Step 3.
Verify Your Identity: Choose a verification method (email, phone call, or text message) and follow the instructions to verify your identity. Once verified, click Next.

Step 4.
Create a New Password: After verifying your identity, you will be prompted to create a new password. The new password must: Be at least 8 characters long (12 or more is recommended). Contain at least one uppercase letter (example: A, B, C). Contain at least one lowercase letter (example: a, b, c). Contain at least one number (example: 1, 2, 3). Contain at least one special character (example: !, @, #, $, %). Not be the same as any of your last 5 passwords. Not contain your name or email address. A strong password example: MyCompany@2026Secure! Confirm the password in the second field and click Next.

Step 5.
Confirm the Password Change: You will see a confirmation message that your password has been successfully changed. Click Done.

Step 6.
Sign Out of All Sessions: To ensure the new password takes effect, sign out of all active sessions on any devices where you are logged in with the old password.

Step 7.
Sign Back Into Outlook Desktop: Open Outlook Desktop on your computer. Outlook will automatically detect the password change and prompt for new credentials. Enter your company email address and the new password you just created. Tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox if on a company device. Click OK. Outlook will reconnect to the server — this may take 1-2 minutes.

Step 8.
Emergency Access for Urgent Meetings: If Outlook Desktop is taking too long to sync after a password reset and you have an urgent meeting or email to access, use Outlook Web Access immediately. Open your browser and go to: https://outlook.office.com. Click Sign In and enter your company email and new password. You have immediate access to all emails, calendar, contacts, and meeting links through the web interface.

issue: I received a suspicious email asking for my company login credentials. I accidentally clicked the link and  entered my password.
resolution:
Problem Summary:
I received a suspicious email asking for my company login credentials and accidentally clicked the link and entered my password.

Likely Cause:
The email was likely a phishing attempt designed to capture and misuse your login credentials.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Change Your Password NOW: Open a browser and go to https://account.live.com/password/reset. Reset your Microsoft 365 password immediately. Do not wait. Every minute of delay gives the attacker more time to use your credentials, access company data, or lock you out of your own account.

Step 2.
Enable MFA Immediately: Go to https://aka.ms/mfasetup. Set up Multi-Factor Authentication if not already active. With MFA enabled, even if the attacker has your new password, they cannot access your account without your phone.

Step 3.
Call IT Security Emergency Line: Call ext. 5911 immediately or email security@company.com with subject: CREDENTIAL COMPROMISE — [Your Full Name] — [Date and Time]. Provide: the phishing email sender address, the website URL you were taken to, what credentials or information you entered, exact time of incident, your employee ID.

Step 4.
Check for Active Unauthorized Access: Go to https://account.live.com/activity. Review all sign-in events. Look for logins from locations you do not recognize, from devices you do not own, or at times when you were not using your computer. Screenshot any suspicious activity for the IT Security report.

Step 5.
Audit Your Email Account: Open Outlook and go to the Sent Items folder. Look for emails you did not send. Go to File > Manage Rules and Alerts. Look for rules you did not create, especially any that forward emails to external addresses. Go to File > Account Settings > Delegate Access. Check if any unauthorized delegates have been added. Report all findings to IT Security.

Step 6.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

Step 7.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

Step 8.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

issue: Our entire finance team of 12 people cannot access  the shared drive since this morning. We have a critical  deadline in 2 hours.
resolution:
Problem Summary:
The entire finance team of 12 people cannot access the shared drive since this morning, with a critical deadline in 2 hours.

Likely Cause:
Multiple team members are simultaneously reporting they cannot access the shared drive, indicating a high severity infrastructure issue such as the file server being down, experiencing hardware failure, or network connectivity disruption.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Check VPN (Remote Workers): Company shared drives are only accessible when connected to the company network. If working remotely, you must connect to VPN first. Open the VPN client and connect. After VPN shows Connected status, try accessing the shared drive again.

Step 2.
Access via Full Network Path: Open File Explorer. Click in the address bar at the top (where it shows This PC or a folder path). Type the full network path to the shared drive, for example: \\fileserver01\company or \\192.168.1.100\shared. Press Enter. If prompted for credentials, enter your company username in format: COMPANY\firstname.lastname and your password.

Step 3.
Re-map the Network Drive: Open File Explorer. Right-click This PC in the left panel. Click Map Network Drive. Select a drive letter that is not currently in use (e.g., Z:). In the Folder field, type the network path to the shared drive. Check the boxes for Reconnect at sign-in and Connect using different credentials if you need to use different credentials. Click Finish.

Step 4.
Clear Saved Network Credentials: Go to Control Panel > User Accounts > Credential Manager > Windows Credentials. Look for entries that contain the file server name or IP address. Click each one and click Remove. Try accessing the shared drive again — you will be prompted for fresh credentials.

Step 5.
If multiple team members are simultaneously reporting they cannot access the shared drive, this is a HIGH SEVERITY infrastructure incident. The file server may be down, experiencing hardware failure, or network connectivity to the server may be disrupted. Do not continue troubleshooting individual workstations. Contact IT infrastructure team immediately at infra-support@company.com or call ext. 5000 and state 'File Server Outage'. This will be treated as a P1 priority incident.

Step 6.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

Step 7.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

Step 8.
[No additional steps provided in the knowledge base]

issue: i had forgetten my outlook password how to reset it
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Employee has forgotten their Microsoft 365 email password and cannot log into Outlook Desktop, Outlook Web Access (OWA), Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, or any other Microsoft 365 application.

Likely Cause:
The employee has forgotten their password, which is required to authenticate and access their Microsoft 365 account.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Open any web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari). In the address bar, type exactly: https://account.live.com/password/reset and press Enter. This is the official Microsoft self-service password reset page. It is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and does not require any assistance from IT.

Step 2.
On the Microsoft account help page, you will see three options. Select 'I forgot my password' and click Next. Do not select the other options unless they apply to your situation.

Step 3.
Enter your Microsoft 365 email address or phone number associated with your account and click Next.

Step 4.
Choose how you want to verify your identity. You can choose to receive a verification code via email, text message, or phone call. Follow the instructions to complete the verification process.

Step 5.
Once verified, you will be prompted to create a new password. Ensure the new password meets all requirements. The new password must: Be at least 8 characters long (12 or more is recommended). Contain at least one uppercase letter (example: A, B, C). Contain at least one lowercase letter (example: a, b, c). Contain at least one number (example: 1, 2, 3). Contain at least one special character (example: !, @, #, $, %). Not be the same as any of your last 5 passwords. Not contain your name or email address. A strong password example: MyCompany@2026Secure! Confirm the password in the second field and click Next.

Step 6.
Sign back into Outlook Desktop: Open Outlook Desktop on your computer. Outlook will automatically detect the password change and prompt for new credentials. Enter your company email address and the new password you just created. Tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox if on a company device. Click OK. Outlook will reconnect to the server — this may take 1-2 minutes.

Step 7.
If Outlook Desktop is taking too long to sync after a password reset and you have an urgent meeting or email to access, use Outlook Web Access immediately. Open your browser and go to: https://outlook.office.com. Click Sign In and enter your company email and new password. You have immediate access to all emails, calendar, contacts, and meeting links through the web interface.

issue: i had forgetten my outlook passward now to reset it
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Employee has forgotten their Microsoft 365 email password and cannot log into Outlook Desktop, Outlook Web Access (OWA), Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, or any other Microsoft 365 application.

Likely Cause:
The employee has forgotten their password, leading to repeated failed login attempts and potential account lockout.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Navigate to Microsoft Password Reset Portal: Open any web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari). In the address bar, type exactly: https://account.live.com/password/reset and press Enter.

Step 2.
Stop all login attempts immediately. Every failed attempt after lockout resets the lockout timer. If you keep trying, the lockout period keeps extending.

Step 3.
Identify what is causing repeated failed attempts. Check your mobile phone email settings — go to phone Settings > Mail and update the password there. Check VPN client saved credentials as described in the VPN guide. Check any other devices (home computer, tablet) where you are logged into company email.

Step 4.
Wait 30 minutes. Account auto-unlocks after 30 minutes of no failed attempts. Do not attempt any logins during this period.

Step 5.
After unlocking, reset your password immediately at https://account.live.com/password/reset to ensure you know the current password.

Step 6.
Update the new password on all your devices — phone, tablet, home computer, VPN client, and any other saved locations.

Step 7.
Sign Back Into Outlook Desktop: Open Outlook Desktop on your computer. Outlook will automatically detect the password change and prompt for new credentials. Enter your company email address and the new password you just created. Tick the 'Remember my credentials' checkbox if on a company device. Click OK. Outlook will reconnect to the server — this may take 1-2 minutes.

Step 8.
Emergency Access for Urgent Meetings: If Outlook Desktop is taking too long to sync after a password reset and you have an urgent meeting or email to access, use Outlook Web Access immediately. Open your browser and go to: https://outlook.office.com. Click Sign In and enter your company email and new password. You have immediate access to all emails, calendar, contacts, and meeting links through the web interface.

issue: My account is locked after multiple failed login attempts
resolution:
Problem Summary:
My account is locked after multiple failed login attempts.

Likely Cause:
Unintentional lockout due to repeated failed login attempts from devices or services using old credentials.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Stop all login attempts immediately. Every failed attempt after lockout resets the lockout timer.

Step 2.
Identify what is causing repeated failed attempts. Check your mobile phone email settings — go to phone Settings > Mail and update the password there. Check VPN client saved credentials as described in the VPN guide. Check any other devices (home computer, tablet) where you are logged into company email.

Step 3.
Wait 30 minutes. Account auto-unlocks after 30 minutes of no failed attempts. Do not attempt any logins during this period.

Step 4.
If you cannot wait, contact IT helpdesk at helpdesk@company.com or call ext. 5000 with your employee ID. IT can manually unlock your account within 5 minutes during business hours (8am-6pm weekdays).

Step 5.
After unlocking, reset your password immediately at https://account.live.com/password/reset to ensure you know the current password.

Step 6.
Update the new password on all your devices — phone, tablet, home computer, VPN client, and any other saved locations.

issue: Emails are stuck in Outlook Outbox and not sending
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Emails are stuck in Outlook Outbox and not being sent.

Likely Cause:
Outlook is experiencing connectivity issues or is in offline mode.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Check Internet Connection: Open a browser and load google.com. If it does not load, you have no internet. Fix the internet connection first using the WiFi troubleshooting guide.

Step 2.
Check Outlook Connection Status: Look at the very bottom status bar of the Outlook window. It should display one of these: 'Connected to Microsoft Exchange' (normal), 'Sending' (normal, actively sending), 'Disconnected' (problem — no connection to server), 'Trying to connect' (problem — attempting reconnection), 'Offline' (problem — Outlook is in offline mode). If it shows any of the problem statuses, proceed to the next steps.

Step 3.
Disable Work Offline Mode: In the Outlook ribbon at the top, click the Send/Receive tab. Look for the Work Offline button. If it appears highlighted or pressed, click it once to toggle back to online mode. The status bar at the bottom should change to 'Connected' within 30 seconds.

Step 4.
Force Send/Receive: Press the keyboard shortcut F9 or go to Send/Receive tab and click Send/Receive All Folders. This manually triggers Outlook to connect to the server and process all pending sends and receives.

Step 5.
Check Attachment Size: If a specific email is stuck in Outbox, it may have an oversized attachment. The maximum attachment size is 25MB. Open the stuck email, check the attachment size. If over 25MB, remove the attachment and share the file via SharePoint or OneDrive link instead.

Step 6.
Restart Outlook: Go to File > Exit to close Outlook. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc), find OUTLOOK.EXE in the Processes tab, right-click and End Task if it is still running. Wait 10 seconds. Reopen Outlook. It will reconnect and process the Outbox.

issue: emails are stuck in outlook outbox and not sending
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Emails are stuck in the Outbox folder and not being sent.

Likely Cause:
Outlook is experiencing connectivity issues or is in offline mode.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Check Internet Connection: Open a browser and load google.com. If it does not load, you have no internet. Fix the internet connection first using the WiFi troubleshooting guide.

Step 2.
Check Outlook Connection Status: Look at the very bottom status bar of the Outlook window. It should display one of these: 'Connected to Microsoft Exchange' (normal), 'Sending' (normal, actively sending), 'Disconnected' (problem — no connection to server), 'Trying to connect' (problem — attempting reconnection), 'Offline' (problem — Outlook is in offline mode). If it shows any of the problem statuses, proceed to the next steps.

Step 3.
Disable Work Offline Mode: In the Outlook ribbon at the top, click the Send/Receive tab. Look for the Work Offline button. If it appears highlighted or pressed, click it once to toggle back to online mode. The status bar at the bottom should change to 'Connected' within 30 seconds.

Step 4.
Force Send/Receive: Press the keyboard shortcut F9 or go to Send/Receive tab and click Send/Receive All Folders. This manually triggers Outlook to connect to the server and process all pending sends and receives.

Step 5.
Check Attachment Size: If a specific email is stuck in Outbox, it may have an oversized attachment. The maximum attachment size is 25MB. Open the stuck email, check the attachment size. If over 25MB, remove the attachment and share the file via SharePoint or OneDrive link instead.

Step 6.
Restart Outlook: Go to File > Exit to close Outlook. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc), find OUTLOOK.EXE in the Processes tab, right-click and End Task if it is still running. Wait 10 seconds. Reopen Outlook. It will reconnect and process the Outbox.

issue: vpn authentication failed after password change
resolution:
Problem Summary:
VPN authentication failed after password change.

Likely Cause:
Old password saved on a mobile phone, VPN client, or another device is causing repeated failed login attempts.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Stop all login attempts immediately. Every failed attempt after lockout resets the lockout timer.

Step 2.
Identify what is causing repeated failed attempts. Check your mobile phone email settings — go to phone Settings > Mail and update the password there. Check VPN client saved credentials as described in the VPN guide. Check any other devices (home computer, tablet) where you are logged into company email.

Step 3.
Wait 30 minutes. Account auto-unlocks after 30 minutes of no failed attempts. Do not attempt any logins during this period.

Step 4.
If you cannot wait, contact IT helpdesk at helpdesk@company.com or call ext. 5000 with your employee ID. IT can manually unlock your account within 5 minutes during business hours (8am-6pm weekdays).

Step 5.
After unlocking, reset your password immediately at https://account.live.com/password/reset to ensure you know the current password.

Step 6.
Update the new password on all your devices — phone, tablet, home computer, VPN client, and any other saved locations.

issue: i clicked a phishing email link and entred my password
resolution:
Problem Summary:
Clicked a phishing email link and entered password.

Likely Cause:
Phishing attack leading to potential credential compromise.

Step-by-Step Resolution:

Step 1.
Change Your Password NOW: Open a browser and go to https://account.live.com/password/reset. Reset your Microsoft 365 password immediately. Do not wait. Every minute of delay gives the attacker more time to use your credentials, access company data, or lock you out of your own account.

Step 2.
Enable MFA Immediately: Go to https://aka.ms/mfasetup. Set up Multi-Factor Authentication if not already active. With MFA enabled, even if the attacker has your new password, they cannot access your account without your phone.

Step 3.
Call IT Security Emergency Line: Call ext. 5911 immediately or email security@company.com with subject: CREDENTIAL COMPROMISE — [Your Full Name] — [Date and Time]. Provide: the phishing email sender address, the website URL you were taken to, what credentials or information you entered, exact time of incident, your employee ID.

Step 4.
Check for Active Unauthorized Access: Go to https://account.live.com/activity. Review all sign-in events. Look for logins from locations you do not recognize, from devices you do not own, or at times when you were not using your computer. Screenshot any suspicious activity for the IT Security report.

Step 5.
Audit Your Email Account: Open Outlook and go to the Sent Items folder. Look for emails you did not send. Go to File > Manage Rules and Alerts. Look for rules you did not create, especially any that forward emails to external addresses. Go to File > Account Settings > Delegate Access. Check if any unauthorized delegates have been added. Report all findings to IT Security.
